summary: 3M's diversification across industrial, healthcare and consumer end markets provides residual resilience, but the shares trade near the top of their 52-week range at a P/E of 25x with structural earnings headwinds persisting - HOLD is the weighted recommendation.

Abbott operates across four principal divisions. Medical Devices covers cardiovascular stents, rhythm management, neuromodulation and Alinity diagnostics platforms. Diagnostics - a legacy COVID-19 strength - includes point-of-care and laboratory-based testing. Nutrition (Similac, Ensure, Pedialyte) serves maternal, infant and adult nutritional needs globally. Established Pharmaceuticals sells generic branded medicines in emerging markets.

AbbVie is a biopharmaceutical company focused on immunology, oncology, neuroscience and aesthetics. Its flagship product, Humira (adalimumab), was the world's best-selling drug for a decade, generating over $20 billion annually at peak, but has faced biosimilar competition in the US since January 2023. AbbVie has managed this cliff through Skyrizi (risankizumab, IL-23 inhibitor) and Rinvoq (upadacitinib, JAK inhibitor), both of which have demonstrated superior clinical profiles in immunology and are growing rapidly.

Ab Dynamics is a precision-engineered niche manufacturer serving the global automotive testing market. Its products - suspension testing rigs, steering test systems, crash simulation equipment, and data acquisition platforms - occupy a defensible position in a specialised B2B supply chain where precision and repeatability are non-negotiable. The stock has de-rated sharply from its 52-week high of 1900 GBX, sitting 41% below that level at 1115.67 GBX, which creates a more reasonable entry point than was available six months ago.

ABVE trades like what it is: a tiny, loss-making small cap without the financial profile to justify much investor patience. At $0.804 with a market cap of roughly $44.8M, the stock sits in the danger zone where modest operational setbacks can matter more than the underlying business narrative.

Accenture is a global professional services firm providing consulting, technology and managed services across strategy, digital transformation, cloud infrastructure, AI implementation and cybersecurity. The company operates across five operating segments: Strategy, Consulting, Digital, Technology and Song (creative/digital advertising).

Accesso Technology Group PLC is a UK-listed niche vertical software company providing queue management, e-ticketing, and point-of-sale systems to the global attractions and leisure sector. The business has successfully transitioned from a hardware-centric model to a cloud-based SaaS platform, and post-COVID recovery in the attractions sector has driven a meaningful improvement in recurring revenue visibility. At 249.28 GBX the stock trades roughly in line with its 52-week range, having recovered from the post-pandemic trough but well off the highs near 400 GBX.

Activeops is a niche UK-listed SaaS business providing workforce management software to contact centres and back-office operations. The company operates in a fragmented market with limited direct competition from listed peers, giving it exposure to the structural shift from spreadsheet-based scheduling to purpose-built WFM platforms in regulated industries.

Adient plc is a high-quality automotive seating manufacturer with leading global market share, but the automotive production cycle is in a soft phase and elevated raw material costs limit the near-term re-rating potential -- HOLD . HOLD Conviction Score: 62/100 - HOLD

Adobe operates three main segments. Digital Media includes Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere, After Effects and the full Adobe suite) and Document Cloud (Acrobat, Acrobat Sign, and the Adobe Express platform). These are sold via annual subscription, generating highly predictable recurring revenues from over 30 million paid members. Digital Experience covers the Adobe Experience Platform, Marketo, and Workfront - enterprise marketing and customer experience management tools.

Ads-Tec Energy makes battery-buffered fast charging systems and related energy management software. The interesting part of the story is not simply EV charging demand, but whether the company can prove that its buffer technology solves grid-connection bottlenecks in locations where conventional ultra-fast chargers are hard or expensive to install. The shares still look speculative because adoption depends on project execution, channel build-out and funding discipline rather than a long record of recurring cash generation.

AdvancedAdvT Limited is a small-cap UK software company operating in the digital transformation and membership management space. FY26 trading update confirmed ahead of market expectations with recurring revenue and margins both climbing — uncommon for a sub-£250m AIM name. The...

Advanced Medical Solutions Group plc is a UK-listed medical device company specialising in advanced wound care and surgical sealing products. Its portfolio spans tissue adhesives, sutures, haemostats, and advanced wound dressings, with manufacturing footprint in the UK and Germany and commercial reach across the UK, Europe, and the United States.

AMD is the most credible alternative to NVIDIA in AI accelerators and has made genuine GPU architecture progress, but at a P/E of 94x the market is already pricing a successful execution of an extremely demanding AI trajectory

AerCap is the largest listed aircraft lessor and its investment case is driven by fleet quality, lease yields, funding access and residual value discipline. The company benefits when airlines want capacity but prefer not to own aircraft outright, especially in a market where manufacturer delivery delays keep equipment tight. The question is less about top-line growth than about how long the current favourable supply-demand balance can support lease economics without a credit or travel downturn intervening.

AIB Group plc is a-systemically important Irish retail and commercial bank, majority-owned by the Irish state following the 2010-era financial crisis recapitalisation. The bank occupies a dominant position in the Republic of Ireland's mortgage and SME lending markets and benefits from a structurally advantaged funding base through the Irish Post Office and AIB's own branch network. The Irish economy continues to outperform the eurozone average, supporting loan book quality, though the housing market remains a key sensitivity.

Albertsons is the second-largest supermarket chain in the United States by revenue, operating approximately 2,200 stores across 34 states under banners including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Jewel-Osco, Pavilions and others. The business model is centred on grocery retail - a low-margin, high-volume model reliant on foot traffic, basket size and operational efficiency. Pharmacy and fuel (gas stations) are meaningful ancillary revenue streams. Private-label brands have been a growing margin lever. The proposed $24.6 billion merger with Kroger was blocked by federal antitrust regulators in late 2024, leaving Albertsons as an independent operator facing intensifying competition from Walmart, Costco, Aldi, dollar stores and discount grocery formats.

Alibaba is a high-quality e-commerce and cloud infrastructure company that has been systematically mispriced by Western markets due to geopolitical risk premium, regulatory overhang from the PRC's tech crackdowns, and US-traded ADR uncertainty. At $126.10 with a P/E of 18.7x and a dividend yield approaching 2%, the bull case - domestic consumption recovery in China, AI infrastructure buildout, and global commerce expansion - is asymmetrically attractive at current levels.

Alkemy Capital Investments PLC is a development-stage investment company focused on critical minerals and battery materials infrastructure. The core asset is the Tavasciute lithium project in Lithuania — a hard rock lithium project with a completed Definitive Feasibility Study and a binding Glencore offtake agreement covering 40% of projected production at competitive pricing. Wates Group has been appointed as the construction partner, FEED is complete, and the project is awaiting Final Investment Decision (FID).

Alkermes is no longer just a pipeline option on unproven science; it has a commercial neuroscience base and the market now judges it on product durability, margin quality and how management redeploys cash. The opportunity is real if the company can keep its marketed portfolio resilient while building the next leg of growth, but investors should recognise that biotech sentiment can turn quickly when key products mature.

Allegion sells mechanical and electronic security products, especially doors, locks, access control and related hardware, with a strong installed-base advantage in institutional and commercial settings. It is a quality industrial, but the investment case usually hinges on whether electronic security growth can outpace any cyclical weakness in non-residential construction. That makes it more of a compounder-at-the-right-price story than a rapid re-rating opportunity.

Allstate is executing a strategic reset. The decision to non-renew 350,000 California homeowners policies is the most significant strategic development - California was a loss-making book due to wildfire risk and legal environment. The company is taking significant cat losses in 2025 and 2026 from Hurricanes Helene and Milton and severe convective storm activity in the central US. The auto insurance combined ratio has been improving but remains above 100% in several states. Rate increases have been substantial but lagged the loss cost inflation. The stock is near its 52-week high of $216.75, suggesting the market is pricing in a successful execution of the strategic reset, but the path is fraught with operational and catastrophe risk.

Alphabet's primary business is Google Search, which processes over 8.5 billion queries daily and generates the majority of group profits through targeted advertising. This is supported by the Google Display Network, YouTube advertising (pre-roll, in-stream and Shorts), and Google Network partner revenues. Google Cloud - encompassing Google Workspace productivity tools, GCP infrastructure-as-a-service, and AI platform services - is the second major revenue pillar and the fastest-growing segment.

Alphabet is the dominant global internet platform: Google Search holds approximately 91% market share, YouTube is the world's largest video platform, and Google Cloud is the emerging third pillar of the business. The company sits at the intersection of several secular growth themes - AI, cloud computing, and digital advertising - but faces the most significant structural challenge to its core search franchise since its founding. The DOJ antitrust victory in August 2024 and the rise of AI-native search alternatives (Perplexity AI, OpenAI Search, Claude-powered search) create a credible scenario in which Google's search ad inventory erodes over the next five to ten years.

Altria's dominant US tobacco franchise provides exceptional cash generation and a 7%+ dividend yield, but secular cigarette volume decline, regulatory pressure and IQOS IPM litigation uncertainty make meaningful re-rating difficult without clearer portfolio diversification --...

Amarin Corporation plc is a Dublin-headquartered specialty pharma company whose flagship product Vascepa (icosapent ethyl) has a credible cardiovascular risk reduction indication but faces significant commercial execution challenges -- HOLD . HOLD Conviction Score: 65/100 - HOLD

Amaroq is a UK-listed investment company that gives London-based investors curated exposure to Iceland's small but high-quality private corporate base. The portfolio spans financial services, real estate, renewable energy, and technology - a deliberately diversified mix that reduces single-sector concentration risk relative to most Nordic micro-caps.

Amazon is the world's largest e-commerce marketplace and cloud infrastructure provider, operating a network effect business with two dominant platforms - marketplace and AWS - that reinforce each other. AWS is the leading cloud platform, generating $100B+ in annual revenue with strong growth and significant AI opportunity through Bedrock. The e-commerce marketplace generates massive cash flow that funds AWS's AI infrastructure investment. The stock at $238.38 is 2% below its 52w high and is the best-risk-adjusted large-cap tech exposure available.

American Express operates a closed-loop payments network - unlike Visa and Mastercard, which facilitate transactions between banks, Amex is itself the issuer and the network. This allows it to capture the full economics of each transaction: interchange fees from merchants, interest income from cardholder revolving balances, and annual fee income from premium card products.

AIG is a complex, restructuring multi-line insurer that has made meaningful progress simplifying its operations and strengthening its balance sheet, but the valuation at low-14x earnings is not compelling given persistent catastrophe volatility and execution uncertainty, warranting a REDUCE.

Amgen discovers, develops and manufactures biopharmaceuticals across several therapeutic areas: oncology (Prolia, Xgeva, Kyprolis, Blincyto), inflammation (Enbrel, Tezspire, Otezla), cardiovascular (Repatha, Evolocumab PCSK9 inhibitor), and nephrology (Aranesp). The company is notable for its pioneering role in the US biotech industry and its substantial manufacturing footprint, which it has leveraged to enter the biosimilar market with Amjevita (adalimumab biosimilar, referencing Humira).

Anglo Asian Mining is a UK-listed gold and copper producer operating exclusively in Azerbaijan under Mining Law agreements with the Azerbaijani government. The company runs the Gedabek gold-copper mine and is developing the Gyzylarahmet gold deposit. At 258.5 GBX, the stock trades at approximately 6x EPS against a gold price environment that remains constructive. The geographic concentration in Azerbaijan is the central risk variable, but the current valuation -- a deep value multiple for a precious metals producer -- more than compensates for geopolitical risk at this stage of the cycle.

Animalcare Group Plc is a focused UK and European animal health business, operating in the veterinary pharmaceuticals, instruments, and nutrition segments for companion animals. The company serves veterinary practices across the UK and Ireland with a product range covering anti-infectives, pain management, and animal welfare products for horses, dogs, and cats.

Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories globally, under the iPhone, Mac, iPad, Apple Watch, and AirPods brands. It also operates a high-margin Services segment comprising the App Store, Apple Music, Apple TV+, Apple Arcade, iCloud, Apple Pay, and a growing advertising business.

Aptiv sits at the intersection of automotive electrification, software architecture and vehicle content growth, but it still operates in a hard car industry where volumes, programme launches and customer concentration can overwhelm the secular story. The appeal is that modern vehicles need more electrical distribution, connectivity and software-defined architecture. The risk is that auto suppliers rarely get paid for vision alone if production schedules wobble or EV demand disappoints.

Arbuthnot Banking Group is a focused UK private banking and wealth management group serving high-net-worth individuals and family clients. It operates through three principal divisions: Arbuthnot Latham (private banking), The Private Office (wealth management), and Elevate Portfolio Management (discretionary investment). The group is characterised by stable fee income, a conservative lending book, and a patient, relationship-led approach to client acquisition.

The fourth quarter of 2025 saw continued volume weakness in North American grain origination as the 2024 harvest was smaller than expected and export demand softened, particularly from China. Ethanol margins have compressed as renewable fuel mandate volumes plateau. The Nutrition segment has delivered resilient revenue growth but faces input cost pressure. Management has maintained full-year guidance but analyst consensus has drifted lower through early 2026. The departure of key senior executives in the prior year created governance uncertainty that has yet to fully dissipate. The stock has recovered from its 52-week low of $44.42 but remains within striking distance of that trough.

AMBP manufactures metal beverage cans and related packaging, supplying beverage producers across multiple markets. This is a scale manufacturing business where value comes from production footprint, customer relationships, plant utilisation, raw material management and disciplined capital allocation.

Arista is one of the cleanest infrastructure beneficiaries of the AI datacentre build-out. Its high-performance cloud networking kit sits directly in the path of hyperscaler capex, and its software-led switching architecture has allowed it to take share from legacy vendors for years. At $145.26, just 0.6% below the 52-week high, the business quality is obvious but so is the valuation.

AST SpaceMobile is a direct-to-device satellite broadband company that connects standard mobile handsets to its space-based network without requiring specialised hardware. The technology is genuinely differentiated and the MNO partnerships (AT&T, Verizon, Vodafone, TELUS, Saudi Telecom) are high-quality. The company has over $1 billion in contracted revenue commitments. The near-term catalyst is the BlueBird 7 satellite launch on New Glenn (~April 19, 2026) — the most important near-term binary event in the portfolio.

AT&T is a major US telecommunications provider, historically operating in two distinct segments: Mobility (wireless) and the now-spun-off WarnerMedia (entertainment). The post-2022 AT&T is a focused telecom business built on three pillars:

Avingtrans is a UK-listed precision engineering group serving the energy, aerospace, and industrial sectors. The business operates across a niche set of specialist manufacturing capabilities - heat exchangers, precision-machined components, and specialised fabrications - with a customer base spanning UK, US, and European markets. At a market cap of approximately £80m, it sits firmly in the UK small-cap universe where liquidity is thin, institutional coverage is minimal, and the discount to net asset value is a persistent structural feature.

Bank of America is a diversified global financial institution organised into four principal divisions: Consumer Banking (deposits, mortgages, credit cards, auto loans), Global Wealth & Investment Management ( Merrill Lynch private wealth and brokerage), Global Banking (corporate lending, M&A advisory, capital markets), and Global Markets (FICC trading, prime brokerage).

company: BIRG - Bank of Ireland Group plc
summary: Bank of Ireland is another core domestic lender whose earnings are currently flattered by higher rates, but the longer-term investment case rests on deposit stickiness, credit discipline and the ability to return capital without inviting a harsher regulatory tone. The bank has benefited from consolidation in the Irish market, though investors still need to separate cyclical earnings strength from truly durable franchise improvement.

company: BK - Bank of New York Mellon Corp
summary: The Bank of New York Mellon is a structurally advantaged custodian bank with fee-based revenues that should benefit from rising interest rates and growing assets under custody, but faces margin pressure and subdued equity markets; HOLD at $127.56.

Berkshire Hathaway is a conglomerate holding company with dominant insurance operations and a disciplined capital allocation philosophy; its intrinsic value growing at above-market rates and significant liquidity make it a compelling long-term hold at $479.90.

Bioventix is a specialised UK biotech that develops and manufactures recombinant antibodies for in-vitro diagnostic (IVD) applications, pharmaceutical partners, and research institutions. The company occupies a niche but defensible position in the antibody supply chain, serving IVD manufacturers who embed Bioventix's reagents into commercial diagnostic kits. Its model is high-margin, recurring-revenue-adjacent, and largely insulated from the binary risk profiles that characterise early-stage biotech.

BlackRock manages $10.5 trillion+ in assets under management (AUM) across equities, fixed income, multi-asset, alternatives and cash. The business model generates revenues from advisory fees (basis points on AUM), securities lending, and the Aladdin risk-management technology platform, which is licensed to institutional investors and other asset managers globally.

Block Energy PLC is a micro-cap oil and gas exploration and production company listed on AIM with production assets in the Republic of Georgia. At a market capitalisation of approximately GBP 15.1 million, the company trades near the bottom of its 52-week range at 1.42 GBX. The company is loss-making with zero reported EPS, rendering traditional valuation metrics inapplicable. Block Energy's investment case rests entirely on the successful execution of Georgian production growth, funding capacity, and the prospect of reserve additions -- a high-risk, binary outcome profile characteristic of micro-cap E&P. The speculative nature of the equity, combined with limited free float and elevated funding risk, supports a HOLD at current levels for all but the highest risk-tolerance investors. Conviction Score: 35/100 -- HOLD.

Bloomin' Brands is a US casual dining restaurant operator with four distinct brands -- Outback Steakhouse (core), Carrabba's Italian Grill, Bonefish Grill, and Fleming's Prime Steakhouse -- operating across the United States and select international markets. The stock trades at a low single-digit P/E on depressed earnings, reflecting genuine operational challenges rather than structural failure. The case for HOLD is anchored in free cash flow generation, an active capital return programme, and a balance sheet that remains manageable -- but there is no compelling re-rating catalyst without evidence of sustained demand recovery in the casual dining sector.

Boeing designs, manufactures and services commercial jetliners (737, 747, 767, 777, 787 Dreamliner), military aircraft (F-15, F/A-18, KC-46 tanker, T-7 trainer, MV-22 Osprey) and space systems (Space Launch System, Starliner capsule, satellite systems). The commercial aeronautics business (BCA - Boeing Commercial Airplanes) accounts for approximately 35-40% of revenues and is the highest-margin segment when operating normally. Boeing Defense, Space & Security (BDS) provides defence platform stability.

Boku Inc is a UK AIM-listed provider of mobile payment and fraud prevention infrastructure serving digital merchants globally. Its carrier billing network connects merchants -- Netflix, Spotify, Disney+ among them -- directly to mobile network operators, enabling payments charged to mobile phone bills. The model is asset-light, high-margin, and structurally advantaged by deep MNO relationships that are difficult to replicate. At 173.25 GBX, the shares trade on a forward P/E of approximately 40x against a business growing revenue mid-teens and generating significant free cash flow. The market appears to apply a UK small-cap/technology discount to what is, in substance, a global fintech SaaS platform with high-quality recurring revenue. BUY conviction: 72/100. Key catalyst is continued margin expansion and potential inclusion in a FTSE index, which would materially broaden the shareholder base. HOLD above 220 GBX.

Booking Holdings is the world's largest online travel agent by revenue, benefiting from structural travel demand recovery and its dominant platform economics; BUY on valuation pullback given strong long-term growth runway.

B.P. Marsh & Partners PLC is a UK-based venture capital and investment company focused exclusively on the financial services sector. Established in 1989 and listed on AIM since 2005, the firm invests in early-stage and growth-stage fintech, insurance, and financial services businesses across the UK, Europe, and North America. The investment approach centres on acquiring minority equity stakes, typically between GBP250,000 and GBP5 million, in businesses with proven concepts but before Series A saturation.

BRCK Group PLC is a nano-cap AIM-listed hardware and connectivity company targeting emerging markets with ruggedised IoT infrastructure. The business has pivoted from a single-product company (the BRCK router) to a broader IoT edge computing and connectivity platform, now encompassing the Kito Cam range and related sensor/connectivity hardware. At 55.00 GBX and a market capitalisation of approximately GBP30 million, BRCK is a speculative proposition: the addressable markets are large and growing, the products fill genuine gaps in emerging-market connectivity infrastructure, and the valuation is accessible. However, the stock carries the characteristic risks of UK micro-caps on AIM: thin trading, limited institutional coverage, questionable liquidity, and a financial disclosure record that makes bottom-up financial modelling difficult.

Brera is a micro-cap multi-club football ownership story. That makes it speculative by default because valuation depends on asset quality, league outcomes, financing and the companys ability to create real economic value from sports ownership rather than just narrative appeal. Investors should treat it more like a high-risk holding company than like a conventional operating business.

Bristol-Myers Squibb is a biopharmaceutical company focused on oncology, immunology, haematology and cardiovascular disease. Key products include Eliquis (apixaban, anticoagulant - BMS share with Pfizer), Opdivo (nivolumab, PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor), Yervoy (ipilimumab, CTLA-4 inhibitor), Revlimid (lenalidomide - multiple myeloma, facing generics), and Pomalone (pomalidomide).

Broadcom operates two distinct businesses: semiconductor solutions (the original Brocade/Semiconductor business) and infrastructure software (following the VMware acquisition, the largest in tech history at $69 billion). The semiconductor segment includes custom ASICs (application-specific integrated circuits for hyperscalers including Google, Meta, Amazon and Microsoft), Ethernet switches and serializers/deserialisers for AI data centre networks, RF components for mobile devices, and Broadcom's proprietary network security and bandwidth management chips.

Burford Capital is the world's largest litigation finance company, providing capital to law firms and corporate clients engaged in commercial legal disputes. It operates a portfolio of legal finance investments, earning management fees, performance fees, and returns on its own deployed capital. The business is structurally long legal market dysfunction and benefits from a growing pipeline of commercial disputes as litigation volumes and values increase globally.

BWX Technologies is a leading supplier of nuclear components and fuel to the US Department of Defence and commercial nuclear sector. With the accelerating militarisation of nuclear deterrence across NATO allies, expanding naval fleets requiring compact reactor technology, and a structural backlog in commercial nuclear new-build, BWXT occupies a critical, difficult-to-replicate position in the western nuclear supply chain. At approximately 28x forward earnings and sitting 8.7% below its 52-week high, the shares offer a compelling risk-reward for investors seeking defence-adjacent exposure with genuine nuclear optionality. The primary investment case rests on sustained federal appropriations for nuclear naval propulsion, the anticipated growth in global nuclear submarine fleets, and incremental commercial opportunities in medical isotope production and advanced reactor components. This is a high-quality, contract-rich business trading at a reasonable valuation relative to its durable earnings base.

Caledonia Mining is a UK-listed gold producer operating the Blanket Mine, an established underground operation in Zimbabwe. The investment case rests on a combination of sub-5x P/E valuation, a 10% dividend yield, and direct exposure to a rising gold price environment. Zimbabwe's Indigenisation Act remains a background regulatory concern, but the company has navigated it successfully to date by maintaining majority ownership. The key variable is cost control at Blanket Mine and the ability to sustain or grow production as the ore body matures.

Capital One has transformed from a credit card lender into a diversified technology-powered financial services company; pending regulatory approval for its Discover merger, it stands to become a payments network major, warranting a BUY rating at $193.00.

Cardinal Health faces a challenging transition as its core US distribution business battles generic deflation and hospital supply chain pricing pressure, while its Medical segment provides modest diversification - the balance of risks tilts toward reduce.

Caterpillar is the world's largest manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, diesel and gas turbines, and diesel-electric locomotives. The company's three primary segments are Construction Industries (compact and heavy construction equipment), Resource Industries (mining, quarry and aggregate equipment), and Energy & Transportation (power generation, reciprocating engines, turbines and locomotives).

Cencora (formerly AmerisourceBergen) offers defensive pharmaceutical distribution exposure with a high-quality specialty pharmacy growth engine, but the absence of reported P/E data and thin public float limit investment precision.

Centene is a major US Medicaid managed care organisation (MCO), serving approximately 17 million members across 26 states. Its model involves contracting with state governments to manage healthcare benefits for low-income adults, children, pregnant women, and individuals with disabilities under Medicaid and CHIP programmes. Premium revenues are received from state agencies (means-tested, largely government-funded), and Centene bears the medical cost risk.

Central Asia Metals operates two polymetallic mines in Kazakhstan: the Kounrad mine, a surface copper and zinc operation in the Karaganda region, and the Sary Arka mine, which produces copper and zinc concentrates. Both mines are surface operations with relatively low cost profiles compared to underground peers, though they remain exposed to input cost inflation and the Kazakhstani tenge exchange rate.

Cerillion PLC is a London-listed specialist in billing, revenue management, and customer experience software for telecoms and utilities. Its pre-paid and post-paid billing platforms, CRM layer, and data analytics suite serve mobile operators, broadband providers, and utility companies across the UK, Europe, and the US. The business operates on a SaaS model with annuity-style recurring revenue, giving it defensible visibility against the more lumpy capital expenditure cycles of its telco client base.

Charles Schwab is the largest US retail brokerage by number of accounts, with approximately 35 million active brokerage accounts, $9 trillion+ in total client assets and 8.5 million Banking and Lending accounts (as of recent filings). Its core model combines brokerage services (equity trading, wealth advisory) with banking products (deposits, mortgages, credit cards) through its bank subsidiary. Post-integration of TD Ameritrade (acquired 2020), Schwab is absorbing a massive platform migration while seeking cost synergies. The business earns revenue primarily through net interest income (on deposit franchises), asset management fees, and trading commissions.

Check Point Software Technologies has been left at the station. While the rest of the cybersecurity sector re-rated sharply post-2022, CHKP has gone nowhere — and today it is sitting precisely at its 52-week low after a -5.15% single-day drop that demands explanation. The valuation is cheap at 14x earnings against a peer group that commands 50-80x. At these levels, a considerable portion of the bear case is already in the price. HOLD is the weighted recommendation: the downside is largely protected by valuation, but the upside requires execution on product transitions that have not yet materialised.

Chevron is the second-largest US oil major, with operations spanning upstream (exploration and production) and downstream (refining and marketing). Key upstream assets include the Permian Basin (the most prolific US shale play, where Chevron is a top-two operator following the PDC Energy acquisition), LNG export facilities, deepwater Gulf of Mexico assets, and international positions in Australia (LNG), Kazakhstan and the DJ Basin (Colorado).

The Cigna Group's combination of Evernorth health services and disciplined Medicare Advantage growth supports a mid-60s conviction score, but medical cost uncertainty and integration execution risk keep it in hold territory.

Cimpress owns online print and mass customisation businesses, most notably Vistaprint, and the debate is whether its platform and customer economics are better than the market assumes for a mature e-commerce print operator. It is more interesting than a simple print label because software, data and fulfilment matter, but it is still a business that has to earn its returns through disciplined marketing and operational efficiency.

Cisco is a classic mature-network-infrastructure business undergoing an AI-era transition. AI infrastructure demand from hyperscalers is driving order acceleration, the 800G switching and Spectrum X/NVIDIA partnership story is real, and free cash flow provides a floor. OPPORTUNISTIC BUY at 57/100.

Citigroup is a turnaround story at a distressed-looking multiple. Q1 2026 EPS beat estimates by 16% on the back of $5.8B in net income, and JPMorgan sees approximately 30% upside to fair value from current levels

Cloudflare is a global cloud infrastructure and security company operating at the intersection of two high-growth markets: edge computing and enterprise cybersecurity. Its core proposition is a single global anycast network spanning 300+ data centres, offering CDN, DDoS protection, WAF, and the Workers serverless platform to enterprises, governments, and developers worldwide. The company is loss-making on a GAAP basis but is very close to free cash flow breakeven, guided for FCF positivity in 2025 - making it one of the last high-quality infra/security growth stories to transition to profitability.

CMR is an early-stage copper and critical mineral exploration company building a portfolio of near-term development assets in Morocco — a jurisdiction it considers mining-friendly, well-located to serve European markets, with attractive permitting and fiscal conditions. The company operates through its 80%-owned Moroccan subsidiary Atlantic Research Minerals SARL (ARM), acquired in July 2023.

Coca-Cola manufactures and distributes alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages across more than 200 countries. Its portfolio spans carbonated soft drinks (Coca-Cola, Diet Coke, Sprite, Fanta), sports drinks (Powerade), juices and value-added dairy (Minute Maid, Innocent), hydration and tea (smartwater, Costa Coffee, Ayataka), and energy drinks (Costa Coffee, Monster in certain markets under license). Coca-Cola operates a largely asset-light model: it concentrates on concentrate production and marketing while independent bottlers -- many of them privately held and operating under franchise agreements -- handle manufacturing, distribution, and local sales. This system provides structural operating leverage and capital efficiency. Geographic exposure is broadly diversified: North America (~35% of revenue), Europe/Middle East/Africa (~25%), Asia Pacific (~20%), Latin America (~15%), and Global Ventures (~5%). The business is characterised by significant pricing power, a recurring revenue model, and strong free cash flow generation that comfortably funds the dividend and share buyback programme.

Cohort PLC is a multi-disciplined UK defence and security group operating through four distinct subsidiaries: MASS (defence electronics and electronic warfare), EID (military communications), SOE (sonar and acoustic systems), and ASV (autonomous systems). The group sits beneath the UK Government's elevated defence spending commitment, benefiting from structural budget allocation to electronic warfare, maritime surveillance, and autonomous capabilities - all areas where Cohort holds established positions with the UK MOD, European defence ministries, and NATO-aligned customers.

Colgate-Palmolive is a defensive consumer staples giant with dominant market share in oral care globally, but faces structural headwinds from private label competition and category deceleration in emerging markets; HOLD at $84.34.

company: CAD  TSX-V - Colonial Coal International Corp
summary: Colonial Coal International Corp is a Vancouver-listed metallurgical (steelmaking) coal company focused on the Peace River coalfield in north-east British Columbia. The company holds a 60% interest in the Steelmaking Coal (SMC) joint venture, a producing asset with long-life reserves and established infrastructure, alongside a portfolio of exploration properties. At $2.77 with a market cap of approximately $514m, it is a micro-cap name that trades on minimal liquidity and thin analyst coverage. The investment case is commodity leverage: metallurgical coal prices have been elevated by global steel demand, supply disruptions from Australia and Mongolia, and structural underinvestment in new coal capacity.

Comcast presents as a deep value trap or a genuine bargain depending on your prior view of the broadband cord-cutting thesis. At 5.5x earnings, the market is pricing that the earnings decline is structural and permanent

ConocoPhillips is a global supermajor with a high-quality, low-cost Permian Basin production base and meaningful LNG upside via its Freeport LNG stake. The Iran peace talks triggered a crude pullback from $131 to $118 — creating a tactical entry point. Jefferies has a $160 price target and Goldman Sachs named COP its #2 energy pick. Q1 2026 earnings on April 30 are the next near-term catalyst. The grok sentiment score of 68/100 reflects constructive energy macro sentiment despite short-term OPEC+ uncertainty.

Cosmo is unusual because it combines specialty pharma assets, medical device exposure and a history of partnering rather than building a huge broad sales force alone. That can create attractive optionality, but it also means the story depends heavily on execution around a relatively small set of products and partnerships. The shares deserve attention for niche innovation, but not blind confidence.

company: First Trust Large Cap Growth AlphaDEX Fund
summary: First Trust Portfolios, the fund sponsor, operates FTC as a single-factor enhanced index fund. The underlying S&P 500 AlphaDEX Growth Index applies a proprietary ranking methodology to the S&P 500 universe, selecting the top-scoring growth stocks based on: (1) price momentum, (2) earnings growth momentum, (3) return on equity, and (4) management change signals. The fund holds approximately 200-300 stocks across sectors, with a deliberate growth and momentum tilt relative to the cap-weighted S&P 500 benchmark. FTC distributes dividends quarterly, has an expense ratio of approximately 0.60-0.70% (confirmed against current First Trust ETF fee schedules), and tracks the index using physical replication. The fund's AlphaDEX methodology is distinct from passive cap-weighted indexing: it systematically overweights stocks with positive growth and momentum signals while underweighting those that score poorly on those dimensions. The result is a portfolio with higher turnover and higher sector active weights compared to a standard S&P 500 ETF, with performance that diverges materially in factor-driven market environments.

This has historically produced the best operating ratio in the North American rail industry. UNP transports diverse freight: intermodal containers (approximately 25% of revenues), agricultural commodities, automotive, industrial chemicals, coal and construction materials. Its network position is highly defensive - the western US rail duopoly with BNSF ( Berkshire Hathaway) is astructural barrier to competition.
